data.title: DevTools Velocity Hack 2026
data.tagline: Ship faster. Debug smarter. Build better tools.
data.description: DevTools Velocity is BeetleX's flagship active hackathon running right now. Over 400 developers are building the next generation of developer productivity tools — from IDE extensions and CI/CD plugins to observability dashboards and local dev environments. This 72-hour sprint emphasizes developer experience, performance, and open-source impact. Live office hours with maintainers from major open-source projects run throughout the event.
data.rules: Projects must target developer workflows. Open-source license required (MIT, Apache 2.0, or GPL). Teams of 2–4. Extensions must be installable and demos must be reproducible. No vaporware — judges will clone and run your project.
data.eligibility: Open worldwide. Professional developers and students encouraged. Teams may include one mentor in advisory role (non-submitting).

tracks.name: IDE & Editor Extensions
tracks.description: Enhance coding environments with plugins, themes, and AI-assisted workflows.
tracks.problemStatement: Build an IDE extension that reduces context-switching by surfacing relevant docs, issues, and deployment status inline while coding.

tracks.name: AI Code Assistants
tracks.description: Integrate AI into developer workflows for codegen, review, and refactoring.
tracks.problemStatement: Create a code review bot that catches security anti-patterns and suggests fixes with one-click apply patches via GitHub/GitLab integration.

tracks.name: Observability & Performance
tracks.description: Build tools for monitoring, profiling, and optimizing application performance.
tracks.problemStatement: Design a lightweight APM tool that traces requests across microservices and pinpoints latency bottlenecks with flame graph visualization.

tracks.name: Web3 Dev Infrastructure
tracks.description: Developer tools for smart contract development, testing, and deployment.
tracks.problemStatement: Build a local blockchain dev environment with one-command fork, snapshot, and reset capabilities for Ethereum testing.

timeline.label: Registration Opens
timeline.description: Early bird registration with bonus office hour slots.

timeline.label: Hacking Begins
timeline.description: 72-hour countdown starts. Discord channels go live.

timeline.label: Submission Deadline
timeline.description: Repos, install guides, and demo videos due.

timeline.label: Judging
timeline.description: Judges clone repos and score live demos.

faqs.question: Can we submit a CLI tool?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. CLI, IDE extensions, web dashboards, and libraries are all valid.

faqs.question: Is there a language restriction?
faqs.answer: No. Use any language. Provide clear install and run instructions.

faqs.question: How does live judging work?
faqs.answer: Judges run your install steps on a clean VM. If it fails, you get one 15-minute fix window.

faqs.question: Are private repos allowed?
faqs.answer: Yes until submission. Make public or grant judge access before the deadline.

faqs.question: Can we continue after the hackathon?
faqs.answer: Yes. Winning projects are encouraged to open-source and maintain post-event.

faqs.question: What is the scoring criteria?
faqs.answer: Innovation (25%), technical execution (30%), developer impact (25%), presentation (20%).

data.title: Neural Nexus Challenge
data.description: Neural Nexus is an active 48-hour hackathon challenging teams to apply machine learning to healthcare, education, and climate solutions. Participants work with curated datasets from partner NGOs and research labs. Emphasis is on ethical AI, explainability, and deployable prototypes. Mentors from Google DeepMind and academic institutions provide hourly office hours.
data.rules: Training data must come from provided datasets or publicly licensed sources. Document data provenance. Teams of 3–5. Models must include explainability outputs. No facial recognition on non-consented data.
data.eligibility: Graduate students and professionals preferred but undergraduates with ML coursework welcome. Teams must include at least one member with Python/ML experience.

tracks.name: Healthcare AI
tracks.description: ML solutions for diagnosis support, patient triage, and medical imaging.
tracks.problemStatement: Build a model that assists radiologists in detecting early-stage lung nodules from chest X-rays. Include confidence scores and saliency maps for explainability.

tracks.name: Climate & Sustainability ML
tracks.description: Predictive models for climate risk, energy optimization, and carbon tracking.
tracks.problemStatement: Create a system that forecasts solar farm output 24 hours ahead using weather and satellite data. Beat the baseline MAE provided in the dataset.

tracks.name: MLOps for Social Good
tracks.description: Infrastructure tools that help NGOs deploy and monitor ML models.
tracks.problemStatement: Build a low-code platform that lets non-technical NGO staff deploy, monitor, and retrain models without writing code.

timeline.label: Registration Opens
timeline.description: Dataset access granted upon team confirmation.

timeline.label: Submissions Due
timeline.description: Model weights, notebooks, and impact report submitted.

timeline.label: Awards
timeline.description: Winners announced with NGO partner presentations.

faqs.question: Are datasets HIPAA compliant?
faqs.answer: Healthcare datasets are de-identified and approved for research use only.

faqs.question: Must models be explainable?
faqs.answer: Yes. All healthcare track submissions require saliency maps or SHAP values.

faqs.question: Can we use pre-trained models?
faqs.answer: Fine-tuning pre-trained models is allowed. Document the base model and training procedure.

faqs.question: Is GPU access provided?
faqs.answer: Kaggle kernels and GCP credits are available to all registered teams.

faqs.question: What is the impact report?
faqs.answer: A 2-page document describing potential real-world deployment and ethical considerations.

faqs.question: Can NGOs adopt our project?
faqs.answer: Winning teams receive introductions to partner NGOs for pilot deployments.

data.title: DevTools Velocity
data.description: DevTools Velocity challenges teams to build ambitious projects across AI, Web3, and developer tooling with mentorship from ecosystem partners.
data.rules: Teams of 2–4. All submissions must include a public repo and demo video.
data.eligibility: Open to students and professionals worldwide. Must be 16+.

tracks.name: DevTools Track
tracks.description: Ship production-ready AI features with measurable user impact.
tracks.problemStatement: Design an AI copilot that reduces developer onboarding time by 40%.

timeline.label: Registration Opens
timeline.description: Teams can register and select tracks.

faqs.question: Can I join solo?
faqs.answer: Teams require 2–4 members. Use Discord matching after registration opens.

faqs.question: What should we submit?
faqs.answer: GitHub repo, demo video, and live deploy URL.
